_notes: Auto-generated by scripts/classify_districts.py (Stage 4) produced an EMPTY rule set: the pack's scraped breadcrumbs are only 3 levels deep (chapter 'Chapter 130 - ZONING' -> article 'ARTICLE VIII. - ZONING DISTRICTS' -> division), and the scraped 'division' breadcrumb text is the literal string 'DIVISION 1. - PERMITTED USES' for EVERY section under Article VIII, even though Municode's own node IDs reveal three real sub-divisions (DIV1 'Permitted Uses' containing the citywide use table, DIV2 'Residential Districts', DIV3 'Nonresidential and Mixed-Use Districts') -- that distinction never made it into the scraped breadcrumb field. So Stage 4's article+division grouping lumped all 18 base-district sections (A-1, R-1 ... R-7, B-1 ... B-4, B-3.5, I-1, I-2, PMD, I-A) plus the use-table/nuisance sections into one heterogeneous 44-source 'ARTICLE VIII / DIVISION 1' bucket and correctly refused to assign it a single district (confidence 0.6, rationale 'governs permitted uses across different zoning districts, without specifying a particular district or use'). Human-reviewed 2026-07-18: like fredericksburg-va (not lynchburg-va), Manassas has exactly ONE section per district (Sec. 130-261 through 130-309) and every section's title literally names its district (e.g. Sec. 130-262 'R-1 low density, single-family residential.', Sec. 130-305 'B-4 general commercial.'), with all dimensional/use/setback/height standards embedded in that same section's body text -- there is no lynchburg-style split into separate Purpose/Uses/Standards sections requiring a numeric-prefix keying strategy. So this pack uses fredericksburg's title_contains-on-district-name approach, scoped with article_contains='ZONING DISTRICTS' (breadcrumb[1], the only breadcrumb level that reliably scopes to Article VIII since division text is uniform). Added one rule for the citywide Sec. 130-241 Use Tables (Tables 1 and 2 list every use type against every base + PD district as columns, relevant to all mapped districts) plus 18 per-section title_contains rules, one per base district. Mapping rationale: (1) AGRICULTURAL -- Sec. 130-261 A-1 agricultural is explicitly named and purposed for 'limited agricultural activities and detached single-family residential development... open space activities and large lot institutional uses' at ~1 du/acre -- mapped to the vocabulary's dedicated 'agricultural' term (used by chesapeake-va, virginia-beach-va, henrico-county-va, prince-william-county-va, christiansburg-va for their equivalent named Agricultural districts), NOT residential-low-density, because unlike lynchburg-va's R-C (which was kept residential-low-density for having no farm-purpose language and an 'R' not 'A' code) Manassas's A-1 has both the 'A-1 agricultural' name AND explicit agricultural-activity purpose text. (2) RESIDENTIAL -- R-1 low density, R-2 moderate density, R-2-S small lots, R-3 townhouses, R-4 manufactured home parks, R-5 multifamily, R-6 age-restricted residential, and R-7 planned residential (Sec. 130-262 through 130-269) all collapse to 'residential-low-density' regardless of density (2.9 to 15+ du/acre) or housing typology (detached/attached/manufactured/age-restricted/planned) -- the closed DISTRICT_VOCAB has no separate density tier, same precedent as every other VA pack in this rollout. R-7 'planned residential' is a JUDGMENT CALL flagged separately below. (3) COMMERCIAL-EMPLOYMENT -- B-1 business office (office-led, 'transition between residential developments and higher intensity non-residential uses'), B-2 neighborhood commercial (locally-serving retail/service/institutional at neighborhood scale), and B-4 general commercial ('wide array of retail, office, service, and institutional uses... serve both local and regional patrons') all map to commercial-employment -- none of their purpose text mentions mixing in residential uses, unlike B-3/B-3.5/PMD below. (4) MIXED-USE-CORE -- B-3 city center commercial (Old Town district) is UNAMBIGUOUS: purpose text explicitly reads 'an attractive and successful mixed-use historic downtown with pedestrian-oriented retail, commercial, and institutional uses along with residential uses at urban densities,' and the section has its own residential-unit standards subsection (130-303(i)). B-3.5 city center planned is also explicit: 'encourage the mixing of compatible land uses... permitting increased residential development at urban densities... extending the architecture and feel of historic downtown,' plus the section bakes in minimum-height rules for 'non-residential/residential mixed-use structures.' PMD planned mixed use development is the least ambiguous of the three -- literally named 'planned MIXED USE development,' purpose text describes 'a balance of... housing... combined with supporting commercial and institutional uses,' and (d) mandates a residential land area ratio requiring 30-60% of the site be non-residential. All three were GIS-verified against real on-the-ground parcels (see parcel_fixtures.json). (5) INDUSTRIAL-ZONE -- I-1 light industrial ('research and development, business offices, light manufacturing, distribution, supply' in the 'Godwin Technology Character Area') and I-2 heavy industrial (higher-nuisance-tolerance, 'heavy truck traffic, excessive noise, and/or unique manufacturing needs') both map cleanly to industrial-zone; the I-1 GIS fixture parcel is on Dean Dr, one block from a 'TECHNOLOGY DR' data-center parcel, directly corroborating the ordinance's own 'Godwin Technology Character Area' framing. JUDGMENT CALLS: (a) R-7 planned residential (Sec. 130-269) -- despite being a flexible 'planned' district like PMD, its purpose text is purely residential ('housing of various types, sizes, densities, and affordability... with supporting institutional uses and services,' no mention of commercial uses, and its Use Table column (Table 1) only carries the same residential/institutional/accessory uses as the other 'R' districts, no COMMERCIAL-category permissions) so it was kept as residential-low-density, NOT mixed-use-core, distinguishing it from PMD/B-3.5 which explicitly split their site area between residential and non-residential. (b) B-3 and B-3.5 -> mixed-use-core rather than commercial-employment: both read as genuine downtown mixed-use districts under the same 'explicit residential+commercial mixing in the purpose text' test fredericksburg-va and lynchburg-va (B-4) used, not a name-based guess. LEFT UNMAPPED (unknown/general): Sec. 130-309 I-A airport district was deliberately NOT mapped to any vocabulary term -- its purpose text ('protect public health, safety, and welfare at the Manassas Regional Airport... provides an area for airport and related business activities... permit maximum flexibility in the mix of uses, intensity of uses') describes a highly specialized, FAA/Airport-Master-Plan-governed district whose on-the-ground uses range from aviation-only (hangars, runways, airside operations under section 130-424's airport protection area rules) to ordinary business uses with 'maximum flexibility' -- unlike PD-MC in fredericksburg-va (a single-dominant-use hospital/medical-office campus that was defensibly mapped to commercial-employment), I-A has no single dominant character, so it was left unknown/general rather than guessed. Sec. 130-242 (public nuisance prohibition) and Sec. 130-243 (mobile retail vehicle prohibition) are cross-cutting prohibitions that apply to every district, not district definitions, and were left unmapped, matching how other packs leave general-provisions sections unmapped. ARTICLE I (Administration), ARTICLE II (Definitions), ARTICLE III Division 1 (Development Standards, e.g. site plans/subdivision/floodplain/accessory structures), ARTICLE IV (Signs), ARTICLE V (Nonconforming Uses/Lots/Structures), ARTICLE VI (Off-Street Parking and Loading), ARTICLE VII (Tree Canopy and Landscaping), ARTICLE IX (Land Use Applications: special use permits, rezonings, historic overlay districts, airport safety overlay, proffers, ARB review), and ARTICLE X (Enforcement) are all genuinely cross-district topical/procedural articles, not base zoning districts, and were intentionally left unknown/general, matching the henrico-county-va/fredericksburg-va/lynchburg-va precedent of not forcing overlay or general-standards articles into the closed vocabulary. GIS verification: City of Manassas's own parcel/zoning attribute service (gis.manassasva.gov/server2/rest/services/Web_ParcelUnit_Standard/Web_Parcel_Standard/FeatureServer/1, field ZONING) was used -- the older manassasgis.manassasva.gov/arcgis21 host referenced in some public search results is unreachable from this environment (connection refused/timeout on every attempt), and the city's ComDev/EnerGov/CRG/CIP folders on gis.manassasva.gov all require an authentication token, so Web_Parcel_Standard's public ZONING field is the authoritative source actually used; see parcel_fixtures.json entries for the four GIS-verified addresses (one per mapped vocabulary term) and their cross-check methodology. Rules are first-match.
